Bayesian meta-analysis workflow

A complete guide using the bayesma package

Source: vignettes/workflow-diagram.qmd

Data preparation

Link Stage 1
Import data
tibble::tibble()
One row per study
Binary / continuous / count

Trustworthiness assessment (INSPECT-SR)

Link Stage 2
inspect_sr() inspect_sr_table() inspect_sr_plot() filter_trustworthy()
Trustworthiness across 4 domains
Plots: inspect_plot() inspect_summary_plot()

Preliminary exploration (PRIMED)

Link Stage 3
primed()
Dependence structure, moderators, ES distribution

Risk of bias assessment

Link Stage 4
rob_plot()
Traffic light plot, domain-level judgements

Model specification & fitting

Link Stage 5
bayesma(sample_prior = TRUE) pp_check()
Priors (μ, τ), RE distribution, likelihood, stage, bias correction

Model fitting

Link Stage 6
bayesma(stage = "one_stage") bayesma(model_type = " ")
Stan MCMC, 4 chains, convergence diagnostics

Model comparison

Link Stage 7
compare_models() compare_plot() compare_table()
LOSO-CV / LOO-IC, calibration, CRPS / ELPD

Model diagnostics

Link Stage 8
diagnostics() pp_check()
Trace plots, Rhat, ESS, posterior predictive check

Bias assessment

Link Stage 9
egger() egger_plot() funnel_plot() robma()
Selection models, PET-PEESE, bias-corrected, RoBMA

Heterogeneity assessment

Link Stage 10
forest() bayesma(re_dist = "mixture") bayesma(model = "robust_outlier")
τ² posterior, predictive distribution

Subgroup and moderation analysis

Link Stage 11
meta_reg() metareg_mod_plot() forest(subgroup = TRUE)
Categorical / continuous moderators, regression coefficients

Sensitivity analysis

Link Stage 12
sensitivity_plot()
Prior, studies-excluded, RE dist., one/two-stage, RoBMA

Interpretation and reporting

Link Stage 13
bayesma() forest() overall_plot() sensitivty_plot() interpret()
Pooled effect + 95% CrI, P(direction), Bayes factor, forest plot, MCMC diagnostics
Left boxes: bayesma functions connects to Right boxes: outputs / configuration

On this page

Developed by Benjamin Moran, Thomas Payne.
